title: To permanently extend the American Samoa economic development tax credit.
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on Ways and Means.
plain_summary: This bill makes a tax credit for businesses in American Samoa last forever. Right now, the tax credit was supposed to end in 2021, but this bill removes that deadline. The tax credit helps companies that operate in American Samoa pay less money in federal taxes. It covers things like worker wages, employee benefits, and business equipment costs.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Ways and Means.

sections.title: Section 1  American Samoa Economic Development Tax Credit Made Permanent
sections.text: This section changes an existing tax credit for American Samoa to make it permanent. The tax credit helps businesses that operate in American Samoa by reducing the taxes they owe to the federal government. Right now, this tax credit has time limits that were set to end on January first, two thousand twenty two. This bill removes those time limits so the tax credit can continue forever. The changes remove the words that limited the credit to the first sixteen years for some businesses and the first ten years for others. The changes also remove the cutoff date of January first, two thousand twenty two. These changes will apply to tax years that begin after December thirty first, two thousand twenty one.
